content.full_text:
CITY OF SAN JOSÉ, CALIFORNIA
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

151 WEST MISSION STREET
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 95110
(408) 277-4454

JOAN R. GALLO
City Attorney

January 7, 1992


Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J. Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

     Re: Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     I am requesting advice on behalf of Councilmember Trixie
Johnson with regards to State disqualification obligations
which may arise from Councilmember Johnson's interests in IBM
Corporation.


Proposed Changes and Their Potential Effects

     Councilmember Johnson is being considered for an
appointment to the City of San Jose's General Plan Update Task
Force. The Task Force will consider the future General Plan
treatment of the Coyote Valley Urban Reserve and Campus
Industrial Area. The Urban Reserve and Campus Industrial Area
comprise several hundred acres in Southeast San Jose. We have
attached a map of these areas and the General Plan sections
describing the current policies on development in these areas.
The Coyote Campus Industrial Area is designated on the map as
"C/I".

     The Task Force will review several options including
expansion or elimination of the Urban Reserve and modification
of its development trigger mechanisms. In addition, the Task
Force may consider whether the average acreage required for
development under the Campus Industrial designation should be
reduced in Coyote Valley.

Scott Hallabrin
Re: Request For Advice
January 8, 1992
Page 2


     The City's Planning Department has informed us that, while
unlikely, the boundaries of the Urban Reserve could be changed
to include some of the industrial area. The elimination of the
Urban Reserve to permit residential development could affect
the value of the adjacent industrial land and its attractive-
ness to developers. In addition, reduction in the average
acreage required under the Campus Industrial designation could
affect the value of some properties in the area.

Councilmember Johnson's Interests

     Councilmember Johnson's husband is employed by IBM. In
addition, she and her husband own 70 shares of IBM stock,
currently valued at over $1,000. During the past year, the
stock paid dividends of over $250.

     In 1974, IBM located a major facility in the northwest
corner of the Coyote Valley when the General Plan designation
for the remainder of the Valley was non-urban. In 1983, the
northern portion of the Valley lying east and south of the IBM
property was designated Campus Industrial and brought into the
Urban Service Area. In the interim, one industrial developer
has built internal infrastructure for a substantial parcel in
the Campus Industrial Area and an improvement district covering
the Area has incurred bonded indebtedness to provide major
infrastructure improvements. In 1984, she middle section of
the Valley was designated Urban Reserve to provide eventual
housing and commercial support for the industrial area.

     The 48 parcels in Coyote Campus Industrial Area are owned
by 17 different landowners. Of those landowners, five owners,
including IBM, own 75% of the area. IBM owns approximately 15%
(243 acres) of the land designated Campus Industrial. IBM is
not listed in the Assessor's Parcel List as an owner of any
land in the Coyote Urban Reserve.


Questions

     We have the following questions:

     1.     Under the facts as presented, would IBM be considered
            directly or indirectly affected by the potential
            changes to the Coyote Campus Industrial designation?

Scott Hallabrin
Re: Request for Advice
January 8, 1992
Page 3


     2.   Does the "public generally" exception apply?

     3.   Will application of the "public generally" exception
          be affected by the fact that IBM is a major employer
          in the area and that many members of the community
          also own shares in the corporation.

     I will be happy to provide you with any additional informa-
tion which you will need to provide the requested advice.
Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

                                        Yours truly,

                                        JOAN R. GALLO
                                        City Attorney

                                        By:___________________
                                           NORMAN SATO
                                           Sr. Deputy City Attorney

JRG:NS:glk
Encs.

cc:  Councilmember Trixie Johnson

sections.question:
We have the following questions:

1. Under the facts as presented, would IBM be considered directly or indirectly affected by the potential changes to the Coyote Campus Industrial designation?

2. Does the "public generally" exception apply?

3. Will application of the "public generally" exception be affected by the fact that IBM is a major employer in the area and that many members of the community also own shares in the corporation.

I will be happy to provide you with any additional information which you will need to provide the requested advice. Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Yours truly,

JOAN R. GALLO
City Attorney

By: [signature]
NORMAN SATO
Sr. Deputy City Attorney

Encs.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: We have the following questions:

1. Under the facts as presented, would IBM be considered directly or indirectly affected by the potential changes to the Coyote Campus Industrial designation?

2. Does the "public generally" exception apply?

3. Will application of the "public generally" exception be affected by the fact that IBM is a major employer in the area and that many members of the community also own shares in the corporation.

I will be happy to provide you with any additional information which you will need to provide the requested advice. Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Yours truly,

JOAN R. GALLO
City Attorney

By: [signature]
NORMAN SATO
Sr. Deputy City Attorney

JRG:NS:g1k
Encs.

cc: Councilmember Trixie Johnson
